mission.title: serverless-workflow: Enforce action name
mission.description: Enforce action name. Requested by 4+ users.

steps.title: Check current serverless-workflow deployment
steps.description:
Verify your serverless-workflow version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=serverless-workflow
helm list -n serverless-workflow 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working serverless-workflow installation.

steps.title: Review serverless-workflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant serverless-workflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=serverless-workflow
kubectl get configmap -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=serverless-workflow
```
**What would you like to be added**:
The name of actions to be `required`.

**Why is this needed**:
The name is a human, comprehensible way of working.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Enforce action name
steps.description:
- Enforce names of states, events, functions, actions, branches and conditions to be required and to follow the [RFC1123](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1123) DNS label format.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=serverless-workflow
kubectl get events -n serverless-workflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Enforce action name" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: - Enforce names of states, events, functions, actions, branches and conditions to be required and to follow the [RFC1123](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1123) DNS label format.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with serverless-workflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
